title: Bridge Essay: Binding action and emotion in first-episode schizophrenia.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Social Incarnation** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Social Incarnation — intersubjectivity, embodied empathy, social predictive processing, second-person neuroscience.

**Binding action and emotion in first-episode schizophrenia.** — Psychopathology. PMID: 25277690. DOI: 10.1159/000366133. Several components of social cognition are compromised in schizophrenia (SCZ) from the early stage of the illness. In this study we first investigated whether mirror neuron-driven embodied simulation (mnES) is altered in first-episode SCZ. Second, we tested whether emotional cues impact on the mnES in SCZ patients. Twenty-two SCZ patients and 22 healthy controls (HCs) observed goal-related actions in either aneutral or emotional context during functional magnetic resonance imaging scanning. Obs...
